codeReview.summary: The feature only partially implements the requested timer-page refactor. It does persist the selected TimerView and adds coverage for view persistence plus running-header elapsed time, but the commit does not split page orchestration out of WorkspaceTimerPage and its new tests do not prove the shared top composer/header state survives view switches or workspace switches as required by the feature description and expected behavior.

issues.description: The feature description explicitly required splitting page orchestration out of WorkspaceTimerPage, but the timer page orchestration still lives in this single ~900-line shell component. The change only adds localStorage-based view persistence at lines 57-89 instead of extracting the shared page-family state into a separate orchestrator/state boundary. That leaves the core refactor requirement unmet.

The new VAL-CROSS-005 test asserts only that the selected view button remains pressed after a workspace switch. It does not prove the required shared top composer/header state or running-timer identity remain shared across view switches and workspace switches, so the expected behavior is under-tested and unsupported by this feature's evidence.

The running-header test checks that the elapsed string changes after 1.5 seconds, but it never samples or asserts the stopped state becomes non-advancing after stop, even though VAL-TIMER-005 requires idle state to stop live advancement. Coverage is therefore incomplete for the documented idle/running distinction.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker handoff claimed `followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript shows it did not perform the skill-required manual browser replay with agent-browser and recorded no interactiveChecks. The skill currently requires a real story-path replay after automated tests for browser-visible features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tracking-frontend-worker/SKILL.md step 10 requires agent-browser replay; handoff verification.interactiveChecks is empty and the transcript contains no agent-browser invocation for worker session 442c155c-bbdc-4b1e-8778-b89780c6fd87.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used non-canonical JS formatting commands (`npx @biomejs/biome ...` and an attempted `npx prettier ...`) even though mission guidance says normal frontend toolchain commands must go through canonical root vp entrypoints. The command surface in services.yaml does not capture a sanctioned formatting lane for this case.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session 442c155c-bbdc-4b1e-8778-b89780c6fd87 shows `npx prettier --write ...` and `npx @biomejs/biome format --write ...`; CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md says JS/TS toolchain commands must run through `vp` or documented root `vp run ...` entrypoints.

summary: Review failed. The commit proves TimerView persistence and updates source-doc status blocks, but it does not complete the required orchestration refactor out of WorkspaceTimerPage and it lacks evidence that shared top-composer/header state remains shared across view and workspace changes.
